text:
A dream written in the stars.

The Excalibur Moonlight places the tourbillon at the very centre of the composition, turning the movement itself into a celestial object. Around it, Murano-technique glass discs carry zodiac constellations, their luminescent details revealed only after dark.

A 45 mm creation in black DLC titanium, born within the Rarities programme, the Maison’s most secret and singular expression of savoir-faire, where the dreams become reality. A unique piece, conceived to bring the night sky to the wrist.

Calibre RD115. Poinçon de Genève certified.

Ref. RDDBEX1221

#RogerDubuis #WatchesAndWonders2026 #WWG26 #Rarities

Written in steel and blue.
 
The Excalibur Biretrograde Calendar brings high watchmaking into a 40 mm creation designed for daily wear. The Cosmic Blue dial deepens in seven layers, from the broad azuré finish of the outer flange to the sun-brushed centre plate, evoking the transition from day to deep sky.
 
Biretrograde hands trace the day and date with immediacy and clarity, continuing a display co-patented by Mr. Roger Dubuis in 1989. 316L stainless steel, three finishing techniques, 14 hand-applied decorations.
 
Calibre RD840. Poinçon de Genève certified.
 
Ref. RDDBEX1209
 
#RogerDubuis #WatchesAndWonders2026 #WWG26

The sky, measured to perfection.
 
The Excalibur Biretrograde Perpetual Calendar distils the most demanding traditions of Geneva watchmaking into 40 mm of pink gold. At the heart of this piece lies the retrograde, the very first signature of the Maison, co-patented by the founder himself in 1989. Its perpetual calendar accounts for months of 28, 30 and 31 days without correction, expressed through a biretrograde display that brings mechanical poetry to the reading of time.
 
An astronomical moonphase, accurate to within one day over 122 years, completes this celestial portrait. Open-worked and structured across nine layers, the dial in Astral Blue transforms these classical complications into something resolutely present.
 
Calibre RD850. Poinçon de Genève certified.
 
Ref. RDDBEX1178
 
#RogerDubuis #WatchesAndWonders2026 #WWG26

Long before borders or calendars existed, every culture on Earth shared the same sky. 

For Watches & Wonders Geneva 2026, Roger Dubuis translates its movements into new horological creations: perpetual calendars, retrograde displays, a central tourbillon, and precious Arthurian chapters where craft and legend converge.

Discover the collection at the link in bio.

#RogerDubuis #WatchesAndWonders2026 #WWG26

High jewellery follows the orbit, with 132 baguette-cut diamonds and rubies set by hand along the circular logic of the display.

A central tourbillon changes the entire grammar of time display, hours and minutes rotating on independent discs around the cage.

Two patent-pending systems were developed to make this possible, placing the flying tourbillon at the center of everything.

Ref. RDDBEX1122
#RogerDubuis #Excalibur #OrbisInMachina

Titanium has its own relationship with light, absorbing it, holding it, releasing it only where the surface demands.

On the Orbis in Machina, the material defines the mood: matte tension giving way to precise, controlled reflections.

The Orbis emerges from the case as if the light itself drew it into focus.

Ref. RDDBEX1157
#RogerDubuis #Excalibur #OrbisInMachina

At the center, fully exposed, the flying tourbillon rotates in complete freedom.

The RD115 was conceived around that singular point, each indication radiating outward in orbit, time constructed from the inside out.

283 components, one central axis, and a logic that repeats itself at every level.

#RogerDubuis #Excalibur #OrbisInMachina

Pink gold holds light differently, warmth accumulating across surfaces, moving through levels, anchoring the composition.
 
On the Orbis in Machina, the material gives the display its rhythm, its depth, its classical resonance.
 
The same architecture, expressed in a more intimate register.
 
Ref. RDDBEX1119
 
#RogerDubuis #Excalibur #OrbisInMachina

The House of Retrograde is the anchor of our future, an enduring code that cuts across our entire identity from the historic Sympathie to the new Excalibur Grande Complication.

By pairing the retrograde with tourbillons and minute repeaters, we continue to prove that calendar complications never have to be tedious.

Stay tuned, the House is on fire.

Ref. RDDBHO0612

#HouseOfRetrograde #RogerDubuis

Hommage La Placide houses the RD1472 calibre with a perpetual calendar and biretrograde display.

With its 38 mm diameter, it recalls the refined proportions of the early years.

Ref. RDDBHO0612

#HouseOfRetrograde #RogerDubuis

Single, bi, or triretrograde, the principle remains unwavering, offering a canvas for endless expressiveness.

Day and date travel along curved ecliptic counters before returning to zero in a precise jump.

A unique rhythm at the heart of the House of Retrograde.

Ref. RDDBEX1174

#HouseOfRetrograde #RogerDubuis

The Excalibur Grande Complication unites a perpetual calendar, a minute repeater, and a flying tourbillon within a single movement.

Certified by the Poinçon de Genève, it integrates a biretrograde display into a calibre composed of 684 hand-finished components.

Ref. RDDBEX1174

#HouseOfRetrograde #RogerDubuis
